title: galium trifidum var. tinctorium
intro: Galium trifidum var. tinctorium, commonly known as Yellow Bedstraw, is a perennial plant that belongs to the bedstraw family (Galium) and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been utilized to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting digestion. In herbal medicine, Yellow Bedstraw is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may be used externally as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
